WALTER L. NOSSAMAN

505 SANSOME STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94111-4712
(415) 398-3600

ALAN M. ELLIOTT
OF COUNSEL

WASHINGTON, D.C.
SUITE 800
1227 25TH STREET, N.W.
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20037-1186
(202) 223-9100

SACRAMENTO
SUITE 1000
400 S STREET
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814-3701
(916) 442-8888

REFER TO FILE NUMBER

July 6, 1992


John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Request for Informal Assistance
    Your File No. I-92-279

Dear Mr. Wallace:

     In response to your request, I have enclosed
information from the Final Environmental Impact Report for the
San Joaquin Hills Transportation Corridor. The enclosed
information shows the limits of the City of Laguna Hills
in relation to the adopted alignment for the San Joaquin Hills
Transportation Corridor.

     If you have a need for additional information, please
give me a call. I apologize for the delay in sending along
this information to you.

                              Very truly yours,

                              Robert D. Thornton
                              of NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX
                              & ELLIOTT

LAW OFFICES

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

WALTER L NOSSAMAN
(1886-1964)

LOS ANGELES
THIRTY-FIRST FLOOR
445 SOUTH FIGUEROA STREET
LOS ANGELES, CA 90017-3802
(213) 633-7800

SAN FRANCISCO
THIRTY-FOURTH FLOOR
50 CALIFORNIA STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94111-4712
(415) 398-3600

LAKESHORE TOWERS
SUITE 1800
(8101) VON KARMAN AVENUE
IRVINE, CALIFORNIA 92715-1007
TELEPHONE (714) 833-7800
FACSIMILE (714) 833-7878

May 4, 1992

WARREN ELLIOTT
OF COUNSEL

WASHINGTON, D.C.
SUITE 800
1227 25TH STREET, N.W.
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20037-1186
(202) 223-9100

SACRAMENTO
SUITE 1000
400 S PINE STREET
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814-3701
(916) 442-8888

REFER TO FILE NUMBER

John W. Wallace, Counsel
Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 8000
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Request for Advice

Dear Mr. Wallace:

This firm is general counsel to the San Joaquin Hills
Transportation Corridor Agency ("Agency"). This letter is
submitted on behalf of Councilman Joel Lautenschleger, a member
of the Board of Directors of the Agency. Mr. Lautenschleger is
also a member of the City Council of the City of Laguna Hills
("City").

This request is based upon the following facts:

1. Mr. Lautenschleger is a member of the City
Council of the City of Laguna Hills. He is the appointed
representative of the City to the Board of Directors of the
Agency.

2. The Agency is a joint powers agency formed by the
County of Orange, the City of Laguna Hills and ten other cities
within the County. The Agency is authorized to plan, design,
finance and construct the San Joaquin Hills Transportation
Corridor ("Corridor") in the County. The Corridor is planned
to be constructed as an eight lane limited access toll facility
between Newport Beach and San Juan Capistrano.

3. In March, 1991 the Board of Directors of the
Agency adopted an alignment for the Corridor and approved
construction of the project. Mr. Lautenschleger did not

NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX & ELLIOTT


John W. Wallace, Counsel
California Fair Political Practices Commission
May 4, 1992
Page 2


participate in this decision because the City of Laguna Hills
has only recently become a member of the Agency.

     4.     Mr. Lautenschleger resides at 24621 Charlton
Drive in the City of Laguna Hills.  Mr. Lautenschleger's
property is approximately 2,000 feet from the adopted alignment
of the Corridor.  Because the adopted alignment for the San
Joaquin Corridor runs along the entire southwestern boundary of
the City of Laguna Hills, a significant percentage of the
population of the City is within 2,000 feet of the adopted
alignment of the Corridor.

     5.     The Final Environmental Impact Report certified
by the Agency with regard to the construction of the Corridor
indicates that noise levels in the area of the Lautenschleger's
residence as a result of the operation of the Corridor will be
below federal and state noise standards.  The EIR also
indicates that traffic levels on the major arterial which
intersects with Charlton Drive, La Paz Road, will be reduced
with the construction of the Corridor, although the EIR also
indicates that La Paz Road will operate at an acceptable level
of service with or without the construction of the Corridor.
Therefore, based on the analysis in the EIR for the Corridor
project, it does not appear reasonably foreseeable that the
construction or operation of the Corridor will result in a
change to the character of Mr. Lautenschleger's neighborhood.

     In light of the foregoing facts, we request written
advice of your office regarding the following issue:

     May Mr. Lautenschleger participate in consideration of
Transportation Corridor related issues by the Board of
Directors of the San Joaquin Hills Transportation Corridor
Agency?

                                   Very truly yours,

                                   Robert D. Thornton
                                   of NOSSAMAN, GUTHNER, KNOX
                                   & ELLIOTT
